fix: c8 load plugin --from correctly identifies newly installed plugin name (#117)

* Initial plan

* fix: preserve correct plugin name when loading multiple plugins via --from

When loading more than one plugin using `c8 load plugin --from`, the
second (and subsequent) plugin installs were incorrectly named because
`extractPackageNameFromUrl` scanned all existing plugins in node_modules
and returned the first valid one found - which was the already-installed
first plugin.

Fix:
- Add `getInstalledPluginNames()` to snapshot valid plugin names before install
- Pass snapshot to `extractPackageNameFromUrl` which now prefers a newly
  installed plugin (not in the snapshot) when identifying the package name
- Add integration test covering sequential --from loads of two plugins

feat: add S3 user storage monitoring template and extend model support

## New config: configs/s3-monitoring/monitoring-s3-users.yaml

Implements the full 'Monitoring S3 Users' template matching the manual
setup documented for zabbixtest3100.

Template structure:
  Discovery rule: S3 Monitoring (key: s3.user.discover, interval: 12h)

  Item prototypes:
    MASTER — S3 Storage data for [{#S3USER}]
      Type: Zabbix Agent
      Key:  s3.user.metrics[{#S3USER},{$S3_USER_PASSWORD},{$S3_USER_GROUP}]
      Value type: text  |  Interval: 12h

    DEPENDENT (JSONPath preprocessing from master):
      - S3 Percentage used [{#S3USER}]     → $.percentageused   (float, %)
      - S3 Total available size [{#S3USER}] → $.totalavailablesize (float, B)
      - S3 Used space [{#S3USER}]           → $.usedspace        (float, B)

  Trigger prototypes:
    - High S3 Storage Limit [{#S3USER}]     severity=high
        problem:  last(percentageused) >= 95
        recovery: last(percentageused) < 90
        manual_close: true

    - S3 user {#S3USER} no data (24h)       severity=warning
        problem:  count(master_item, #2) = 0
        recovery: count(master_item, #2) > 0
        manual_close: true

## Model extensions (zbx/models.py)

- ItemType: added 'dependent' (Zabbix API type 18)
- PreprocessingType enum: jsonpath, regex, multiplier, trim, etc.
  with zabbix_id property mapping to Zabbix integer codes
- Preprocessing model: type, params, error_handler, error_handler_params
- ItemPrototype: added master_item_key (str) and preprocessing (list)
- TriggerPrototype model: name, expression, severity, recovery_expression,
  description, allow_manual_close, enabled, tags
- DiscoveryRule: added trigger_prototypes list

## Client extensions (zbx/zabbix_client.py)

- get_trigger_prototypes(ruleid)
- create_trigger_prototype(data)
- update_trigger_prototype(triggerid, **kwargs)

## Deployer extensions (zbx/deployer.py)

- _create_item_prototype: now builds preprocessing array, handles
  master_itemid injection for dependent items, returns created itemid
- _create_discovery_rule: two-pass prototype creation — non-dependent
  first (builds key→itemid map), then dependent with master resolution
- _create_trigger_prototype: creates trigger prototypes with
  recovery_expression and manual_close support

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(network): restore relay_server to clients — every P2P node is a relay

In a decentralised P2P network every node should relay for others.
Removing relay_server was architecturally wrong.

The real fix for 4G cycling is the Identify::Received timing change
already in place — that ensures the relay_client reservation lands on
the surviving connection handler after deduplication, so
connection_keep_alive() returns true regardless of relay_server state.

Triggered when users mention 'remote', 'on a codespace', 'codespace tools',
or specific tool names like 'remote_bash'.

Includes full reference for all 13 MCP tools, tool routing guide,

* feat: registry, workspace, provisioner packages and MCP multi-codespace refactor

Phase 1: Codespace Registry
- New internal/registry package with ManagedCodespace, Registry (map[alias]*ManagedCodespace)
- Resolve() auto-selects when single CS, requires alias when multiple
- DefaultAlias() derives alias from repo name with conflict resolution

Phase 1.2: MCP server refactored to use Registry

- NewServerSingle() convenience wrapper for backward compatibility

- TerminfoProvisioner: uploads terminfo for non-standard terminals
- GitFetchProvisioner: git fetch + optional branch checkout

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: lifecycle tools, multi-codespace flags, remove shell patch, provisioner config

Phase 2.1: Multi-codespace flag parsing

Phase 3.2-3.4: Lifecycle MCP tools
- create_codespace: creates CS via gh CLI, waits for SSH, deploys agent, registers
- connect_codespace: connects to existing CS, sets up SSH, registers
- delete_codespace: deregisters, optionally deletes from GitHub
- GHRunner interface for testing gh CLI interactions

Phase 4.1: Remove shell patch
- Delete execCopilotWithShellPatch, findCopilotIndexJS, readCopilotToken
- Remove --local-shell flag

Phase 6.2: User-defined provisioner config

- Each CS gets: start, detect workdir, SSH setup, deploy exec, register

* fix: add list_available_codespaces tool for local gh cs list

The agent couldn't discover other codespaces because remote_bash runs

* fix: SSH multiplexing retry and better error diagnostics

- Retry ControlMaster connection once with 3s delay before falling back.
  Freshly-started codespaces often need a moment for the tunnel to
  stabilize.
- Capture SSH stderr in error message instead of just 'exit status 255'.
  Now shows the actual SSH error for easier debugging.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add --local-bash flag to keep local bash tool enabled

When --local-bash is passed, the local bash tool is not excluded,
allowing the agent to run commands locally alongside remote_bash
on codespaces. Useful for running local gh commands, file operations,
or other tools that need local execution.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: generate remote-explorer custom agent for codespace exploration

Built-in explore agents use local grep/glob/view which are excluded,
so they can't find files on the codespace. This generates a custom
agent in the mirror directory that has access to all codespace/* MCP
tools.

- remote-explorer.agent.md generated in .github/agents/ on every
  session (launcher + resume)
- Uses claude-haiku-4.5 for fast exploration
- Tools: codespace/*, read, search
- Instructions preamble updated to recommend @remote-explorer
  for codebase exploration instead of built-in explore agent
- Integration test verifies agent file generation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent MCP timeouts for long-running commands

- Remove mutex from ssh.Client.Exec() — SSH multiplexing handles
  concurrency at the transport level, and the mutex caused head-of-line
  blocking where a long sync command blocked all other tool calls
- Update remote_bash description to recommend async mode for commands
  >30 seconds (builds, tests, benchmarks)
- Add timeout hint in bash error response when context is cancelled
- Update create_codespace description noting 1-3 minute duration
- Update connect_codespace description noting 30-60 second duration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add initial_wait to remote_bash for long-running sync commands

Matches the local bash tool's initial_wait behavior: when provided in
sync mode, starts the command in a tmux session, waits up to
initial_wait seconds, then returns partial output + shellId for
follow-up reads with remote_read_bash.

This bridges the gap between pure sync (blocks until done, may timeout)
and full async (returns immediately). Example: initial_wait=120 for
builds waits up to 2 minutes, returning output + shellId if not done.

Also:
- Improved remote_read_bash description with polling/backoff guidance
- Updated timeout hint to mention initial_wait as an option
- Updated remote-tools skill with initial_wait documentation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: probe and recover broken IDE socket forwards

After setting up SSH socket forwarding for IDE connections, probe
the local socket to verify it actually works. If ECONNREFUSED:

1. Cancel the dead forwarding
2. Re-fetch IDE lock files from codespace (VS Code may have
   restarted with a new socket path)
3. Retry forwarding with the fresh socket path
4. If retry also fails, clean up and skip (no dead lock file)

Also:
- Extract CancelForward() from ForwardSocket() for reuse
- Add probeSocket() using net.DialTimeout for quick validation

Fixes: IDE connection errors after VS Code restarts or tunnel drops.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: IDE file selection uses remote workdir, forward all codespaces

Two fixes for IDE integration:

1. WorkspaceFolders in the forwarded lock file now uses the remote
   codespace workdir (e.g., /workspaces/repo) instead of the local
   mirror dir. When VS Code sends a selected file path, copilot
   receives a path that matches remote_view expectations.

2. Forward IDE connections from ALL connected codespaces, not just
   the primary one. Each codespace's VS Code instance gets its own
   forwarded socket with its own remote workdir.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: refresh IDE auth nonce when VS Code restarts

When VS Code restarts, it generates a new authorization nonce but
may keep the same socket path. The SSH forwarding still works (probe
passes) but the local lock file has the stale nonce, causing 'socket
hang up' when copilot tries to authenticate.

Fix: always re-fetch the remote lock file after a successful socket
probe to get the latest headers (nonce). Also update headers in the
retry path when socket path hasn't changed but nonce has.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: strip tmux "Pane is dead" decoration from remote_bash output

The remain-on-exit tmux option causes a "Pane is dead (status N, ...)"
decoration line to appear in the pane. capture-pane captures this as
part of the output, leaking tmux internals to the user.

Add cleanPaneOutput() to strip this decoration and trailing blank lines
from captured pane content before returning it via ReadSession().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(launcher): rename --local-bash to --local-tools

When --local-tools is set, keep all 7 local tools (bash, write_bash,
read_bash, stop_bash, list_bash, grep, glob) enabled alongside the
remote_* MCP tools. Previously --local-bash only kept bash enabled.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: initial implementation of zbx — Zabbix configuration as code CLI

Add a production-ready Python CLI tool that manages Zabbix monitoring
configuration as YAML files deployable through a Git workflow.

## Architecture

zbx/
  models.py          — Pydantic v2 models: Template, Item, Trigger,
                        DiscoveryRule, ItemPrototype. All Zabbix API integer
                        IDs are mapped bidirectionally through typed enums
                        (TriggerSeverity, ItemType, ItemValueType).

  config_loader.py   — Loads YAML files (single file or recursive directory
                        scan). Validates every document against the Pydantic
                        schema and surfaces clear error messages on failure.

  zabbix_client.py   — Thin JSON-RPC HTTP client built on requests. Handles
                        authentication (Zabbix 5.4+ and legacy user.login),
                        context-manager login/logout, and full CRUD for
                        templates, items, triggers, discovery rules and item
                        prototypes. Includes find_templates() for the export
                        command.

  diff_engine.py     — Computes a structured TemplateDiff between the
                        desired YAML state and the current Zabbix state.
                        Produces ChangeType-tagged ResourceChange objects
                        with FieldChange lists (old_value → new_value) for
                        items, triggers and discovery rules.

  deployer.py        — Applies diffs via zabbix_client. Supports dry-run
                        mode. Never auto-deletes resources — removals emit a
                        warning and require manual action (safe default).
                        All operations are idempotent.

  formatter.py       — Rich-based Terraform-style output: green + for adds,
                        yellow ~ for modifications, red - for removals.
                        Includes plan summary and apply result table.

  commands/
    apply.py         — Shows plan, prompts for confirmation, applies with
                        progress spinner. Flags: --dry-run, --auto-approve.
    plan.py          — Dry-run diff against live Zabbix.
    diff.py          — Alias of plan; matches infra-engineer muscle memory.
    validate.py      — Schema validation without API connection.
    export.py        — Fetches a template from Zabbix and serialises it to
                        YAML (migration path from web UI to Git).

  cli.py             — Typer app. Registers all five commands. Global
                        --verbose/-v flag configures RichHandler logging.
                        --version/-V prints the package version.

## Example configs

configs/templates/linux-observability.yaml
  6 items (CPU, memory, load, processes, uptime)
  4 triggers (high CPU disaster/warning, memory average/high)
  2 LLD rules (filesystem discovery, network interface discovery)
  with item prototypes for each

configs/templates/nginx.yaml
  4 items (status page, active connections, request rate, process count)
  2 triggers (process down → disaster, high request rate → warning)

## Workflow

  zbx validate configs/          # validate YAML schema
  zbx plan     configs/          # dry-run: show what would change
  zbx diff     configs/          # diff local config ↔ live Zabbix
  zbx apply    configs/          # apply with confirmation prompt
  zbx apply    configs/ -y       # apply non-interactively (CI/CD)
  zbx export   linux-server      # export existing template to YAML

## Configuration

Connection settings are read from environment variables or a .env file:
  ZBX_URL, ZBX_USER, ZBX_PASSWORD, ZBX_VERIFY_SSL, ZBX_TIMEOUT

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add RecommendedReaderVersion advisory mechanism to cDAC RuntimeInfo contract (#125029)

…ontract

Adds a new CDAC_RECOMMENDED_READER_VERSION constant and
RecommendedReaderVersion global to datadescriptor.inc. The runtime team
can increment this value when making changes where updating diagnostic
tools is recommended. This is purely advisory and does not necessarily
mean a breaking change occurred.

The RuntimeInfo contract exposes two new properties:
- CurrentReaderVersion: a compile-time constant representing the reader
version this contract was authored against (currently 1)
- RecommendedReaderVersion: reads the runtime global of the same name

Diagnostic tools can compare these to determine whether to recommend an
update to the user. When the runtime ships a higher
RecommendedReaderVersion than a tool's CurrentReaderVersion, the tool
knows newer contracts are available.

---------

commit.message:
fix: prevent webhook panic on auto-generated vLLM presets with empty GPU params (#1824)

## Problem

For auto-generated vLLM presets (any HuggingFace model not in KAITO's
built-in preset list, e.g. `Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B`), `GPUCountRequirement` is
always an empty string because the generator has no logic to derive GPU
count from HuggingFace metadata. Calling `resource.MustParse("")` on
this empty string caused the webhook to panic with:

```
cannot parse '': quantities must match the regular expression...
```

This crashes the webhook and returns a 500 error to the user on
workspace creation.

## Root Cause

- Built-in presets hardcode both `GPUCountRequirement` and
`TotalSafeTensorFileSize`
- Auto-generated vLLM presets only populate `TotalSafeTensorFileSize`
(from HuggingFace model metadata) but never set `GPUCountRequirement`
- The validation code called `resource.MustParse()` on both fields
without checking for empty strings

## Fix

- **Independent field checks**: Instead of a single all-or-nothing OR
guard, check each field independently. GPU count validation is skipped
only when `GPUCountRequirement` is empty. GPU memory + distributed
inference checks are skipped only when `TotalSafeTensorFileSize` is
empty.
- **Safe parsing**: Replace `resource.MustParse` with
`resource.ParseQuantity` with proper error handling to prevent panics on
malformed input.
- **Both API versions**: Applied to both `v1alpha1` and `v1beta1`
workspace validation.
- **Warning logs**: Log warnings when skipping individual checks due to
empty or unparseable fields.

## Testing

Deployed the fix and validated that creating a workspace with
`Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B` (a non-built-in model) no longer panics the webhook.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Andy Zhang <xiazhang@microsoft.com>

fix: nil concat crash on empty date and jobstart chunk buffering

ui.lua: comment.createdAt:match() can return nil when createdAt is ""
(our fallback value). Concatenating nil crashes with a Lua error.
Guard both the empty-string case and the nil match result.

github.lua: jobstart delivers stdout in chunks where the last element
of each chunk is a partial line joined to the first element of the next
chunk. The previous code dropped these boundaries, corrupting large JSON
responses (e.g. PRs with many comments). Rewritten with a running
string buffer that correctly reconstructs the full output.

- scripts/translate_ha_config.py: maps log_level → LOG_LEVEL
- sendspin_client.py: applies level to root logger at startup,
  sets LOG_LEVEL env var so subprocesses inherit it

  handles new set_log_level stdin command for runtime changes
- routes/api.py: POST /api/settings/log_level — applies immediately
  to main process and all running subprocesses, persists to config.json
- static/app.js + templates/index.html: log level select in Advanced
  settings with Apply now button (no restart required)
- Bump version 2.7.3 → 2.7.4

commit.message:
fix: replace vim.system with jobstart, fix nvim_win_set_option crash

vim.system was added in Neovim 0.10 — any older version crashed on the
first async call. Replaced with vim.fn.jobstart which works from 0.5+.

nvim_win_set_option is deprecated in 0.10 and removed in newer builds.
Replaced with nvim_set_option_value (available since Neovim 0.8).

Also add or-defaults (or 80 / or 20) on max_comment_width/height so
the UI doesn't crash if setup() hasn't been called yet.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
